I couldn't find any information about a plant called Spiesia gymnogyne. However, I found a plant called Spigelia marilandica, also known as Indian pink, American snuff box, or Worm-grass, and it seems to be closely related to the genus Spiesia (Spigelia is a member of the Loganiaceae family and Spiesia is a member of the Lamiaceae family, both of which were once classified under the same Loganiaceae family). Here's a paragraph about Spigelia marilandica:

Spigelia marilandica, also known as Indian pink, is a flowering plant native to North America, belonging to the family Loganiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The bioactive constituents of Spigelia marilandica include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its health benefits. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are tinctures and infusions, often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
